ucs.title: [LLM] APT39 (Chafer) ASPXSpy webshell + Plink/Ngrok reverse-tunnel for RDP pivot
ucs.description: Hunts APT39's signature post-exploitation chain on internet-facing IIS/Exchange: drop ASPXSpy (or variant) webshell, then use w3wp.exe to launch Plink/Ngrok with reverse-tunnel flags to expose internal RDP (3389) outbound, enabling MOIS operators to pivot to tracked targets in travel/telecom/hospitality networks.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_name) as process_name values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent_process_name values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="httpd.exe") (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","plink.exe","ngrok.exe","frpc.exe","net.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","netstat.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*-R *:127.0.0.1:3389*","*-L 3389*","*tcp 3389*","*aspxspy*","*Server=AspxSpyMembers*")) by Processes.dest Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_id _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=left dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count as tunnel_egress values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as egress_ports from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_category!="internal" (All_Traffic.dest_port IN (22,443,4443,7547) OR All_Traffic.app IN ("ngrok","ssh","plink")) by All_Traffic.src | rename All_Traffic.src as dest] | where tunnel_egress>0 OR match(process,"(?i)(-R\s+\d+:|aspxspy|ngrok\s+tcp|plink.*-pw|/usr/share/aspxspy)") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql: let webshellHosts = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe") | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","net.exe","whoami.exe","netstat.exe","systeminfo.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe") or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("AspxSpy","AspxSpyMembers","FilesAntsword","SilverSHell") | project ShellTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, ShellCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ShellParent=InitiatingProcessFileName, ShellChild=FileName; let tunnelProcs = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where (FileName in~ ("plink.exe","ngrok.exe","frpc.exe","frps.exe","chisel.exe") or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-R\s+\d+:(127\.0\.0\.1|localhost):3389" or ProcessCommandLine has_all ("ngrok","tcp","3389") or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)plink.*-pw\s+\S+.*-R") | project TunnelTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, TunnelCmd=ProcessCommandLine, TunnelImage=FileName, TunnelUser=AccountName; webshellHosts | join kind=inner (tunnelProcs) on DeviceId | where TunnelTime between (ShellTime .. ShellTime + 6h) | join kind=leftouter (DeviceNetworkEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where RemotePort in (22,443,4443,7547) and RemoteIPType=="Public" | summarize EgressIPs=make_set(RemoteIP,25), EgressPorts=make_set(RemotePort,10) by DeviceId) on DeviceId | project ShellTime, TunnelTime, DeviceName, ShellParent, ShellChild, ShellCmd, TunnelImage, TunnelCmd, TunnelUser, EgressIPs, EgressPorts | order by ShellTime desc
ucs.rationale: ASPXSpy is APT39/Chafer's documented webshell of choice (FireEye/Mandiant Jan-2019 and Symantec Dec-2015 reporting), and the same intrusions consistently chain w3wp.exe child processes into Plink/Ngrok reverse tunnels exposing internal RDP — used to surveil airline, telco and hospitality PII in line with MOIS targeting. The two-stage join (webshell-spawned recon → outbound tunnel binary on the sam

ucs.title: [LLM] APT39 Remexi/POWBAT browser credential harvest with BITS staging and Run-key persistence
ucs.description: Hunts the Remexi/POWBAT post-compromise pattern: AutoIt or PowerShell loader reads Chrome/IE 'Login Data' and cookie SQLite files, exfils via bitsadmin/Start-BitsTransfer, and persists through HKCU\...\Run — APT39's standard kit for harvesting webmail/social creds of MOIS targets of interest.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.process_name) as opener from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name IN ("Login Data","Cookies","Web Data","Local State") OR Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\WebCache\\WebCacheV01.dat","*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*\\logins.json","*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*\\key4.db")) Filesystem.process_name!="chrome.exe" Filesystem.process_name!="msedge.exe" Filesystem.process_name!="firefox.exe" Filesystem.process_name!="iexplore.exe" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count as bits_count values(Processes.process) as bits_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("bitsadmin.exe","powershell.exe") AND (Processes.process IN ("*Start-BitsTransfer*","*bitsadmin*/transfer*","*bitsadmin*/addfile*","*bitsadmin*/upload*"))) by Processes.dest | rename Processes.dest as dest] | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count as run_key_writes values(Registry.registry_path) as reg_path values(Registry.registry_value_data) as reg_value from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path IN ("*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\*","*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce\\*") (Registry.registry_value_data IN ("*AutoIt*","*\\AppData\\*\\*.exe","*powershell*-enc*","*\\ProgramData\\*.exe","*\\Public\\*.exe","*Remexi*")) by Registry.dest | rename Registry.dest as dest] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime dest user opener file_path bits_cmd reg_path reg_value
ucs.kql: let credAccess = DeviceFileEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where FileName in~ ("Login Data","Cookies","Web Data","Local State","logins.json","key4.db","WebCacheV01.dat") | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","brave.exe","MsMpEng.exe","SenseIR.exe") | project CredTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, BrowserFile=FileName, FilePath=FolderPath, CredProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, CredCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine, CredUser=InitiatingProcessAccountName; let bits = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where (FileName =~ "bitsadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/transfer","/addfile","/upload","/setnotifycmdline")) or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "Start-BitsTransfer") | project BitsTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, BitsCmd=ProcessCommandLine; let runKey = DeviceRegistryEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where RegistryKey has_any (@"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce") | where RegistryValueData has_any ("AutoIt",".exe","powershell","-enc","\\AppData\\","\\ProgramData\\","\\Public\\","Remexi","rundll32") | where RegistryValueData !has "OneDrive" and RegistryValueData !has "Teams" | project RegTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RegKey=RegistryKey, RegValName=RegistryValueName, RegValData=RegistryValueData; credAccess | join kind=inner (bits) on DeviceId | where BitsTime between (CredTime - 1h .. CredTime + 6h) | join kind=inner (runKey) on DeviceId | where RegTime between (CredTime - 24h .. CredTime + 24h) | summarize CredAccessSamples=make_set(strcat(BrowserFile,"|",CredProc),10), BitsSamples=make_set(BitsCmd,5), PersistenceSamples=make_set(strcat(RegValName,"=",RegValData),5) by DeviceName, DeviceId, CredUser, bin(CredTime,1h) | order by CredTime desc
ucs.rationale: Kaspersky's Remexi reporting and FireEye's APT39 dossier both highlight that Chafer/APT39 specifically pairs browser credential file reads (Login Data, IE WebCache, Firefox key4.db) with BITS-based exfiltration and HKCU Run-key persistence pointing to AutoIt or PowerShell stagers — this is the unit-of-work used to track airline, telco and academic targets for MOIS. Requiring all three signals on t
